mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts are not offset by accelerating CPIAUCSL.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX forward returns are not positive more than 55% of the time during FEDFUNDS downtrends, or if the signal disappears when CPIAUCSL is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply, investors price weaker household income and lower corporate sales, pushing S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when PAYEMS growth is also slowing.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the hit rate is unchanged when PAYEMS is flat or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A joint upswing in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening domestic demand and stronger nominal revenue growth, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is not expected to work if payroll gains occur without retail sales acceleration.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-RSAFS signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or if positive 20d returns do not cluster when both series improve together.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or lower CPIAUCSL implies improving real output without renewed inflation pressure, which should lift equities over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when industrial production rises alongside accelerating prices because margin pressure offsets growth.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d SPX forward returns are not above baseline when INDPRO improves and CPIAUCSL is flat-to-lower, or if the relationship flips positive during inflation acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST above trend signals future household formation and construction-linked demand, which supports equities over the next quarter through earnings expectations in cyclicals and materials. The effect should weaken when mortgage-sensitive demand is already collapsing, but that is not observable here.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing upturns are followed by negative 60d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if no improvement is observed relative to non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already elevated, any additional increase tightens discount rates and credit conditions, making S&P 500 returns negative over the next 5 trading days. The effect is fastest when inflation is not falling.
failure_condition: Reject if five-day forward returns are not negative in a majority of high-rate episodes, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for CPIAUCSL not decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deterioration in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE indicates labor-market stress, reducing expected consumption and increasing recession odds, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal requires both conditions together rather than either one alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined deterioration signal does not beat either single-series proxy in hit rate, or if triggered episodes yield positive 20d returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth with improving retail sales indicates broad final demand that supports corporate revenue expectations and pushes the S&P 500 higher over the next 60 trading days. The signal should be strongest after weak periods, when marginal demand surprises are larger.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d SPX forward returns are not positive in most episodes where GDP and retail sales both improve, or if the interaction has no incremental value over either series alone.
